summary: Freeze the v1 architecture as a layered SKU analysis service: a consumer-facing API surface calls an analysis application layer, which orchestrates ERP-only source access, source normalization, canonical SKU and local-warehouse mapping, and metric derivation. Validation and evidence run as a sidecar layer that can inspect raw, normalized, derived, and assembled outputs without widening the source boundary beyond ERP APIs.

moduleDefinitions.name: API Surface
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Own the outward v1 request and response contract, input validation, response serialization, and consumer-facing endpoint boundaries for SKU analysis queries.

moduleDefinitions.name: Analysis Application
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Coordinate SKU analysis use cases, request windows and as-of dates, call the ERP source and domain layers in the right order, and assemble the final API response without owning ERP-specific transport details or business derivation logic.

moduleDefinitions.name: ERP Source Gateway
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Own all ERP API access, authentication, pagination, retry policy, raw endpoint request/response models, and source-specific fetch operations for SKU references, inventory snapshots, warehouse quantities, movement events, and sales events.

moduleDefinitions.name: Source Normalization
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Translate raw ERP payloads into normalized internal records with explicit provenance, business timestamps, quantities, and warehouse identifiers, without yet freezing unresolved canonical joins as facts.

moduleDefinitions.name: Canonical Mapping
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Resolve the canonical SKU key across ERP API surfaces and decide how the default local warehouse is identified, including default_local_wid or explicit local-warehouse mapping when required.

moduleDefinitions.name: Metric Derivation
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Produce daily facts and required business metrics from normalized records after canonical mapping, including 30-day outbound, 30/60/90-day sales windows, and turnover days with traceable derivation metadata.

moduleDefinitions.name: Validation And Evidence
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Run representative-SKU validation, inspect raw-to-derived lineage, emit discrepancy reports, and prove boundary integrity without adding an alternate production source path.

dependencyRules.rationale: The API layer should delegate orchestration to the application layer instead of owning business flow.
Consumer-facing endpoints must not call ERP APIs directly.
The API surface must not compute business metrics or joins.
The application layer orchestrates ERP data retrieval through the single approved source gateway.
The application layer must normalize raw ERP payloads before any business derivation.
Canonical SKU and local-warehouse decisions are orchestration dependencies for building a correct response.
The application layer assembles the final response from derived business metrics.
Normalization depends on the raw ERP source models owned by the gateway.
Normalization should produce normalized records only and must not derive business metrics.
Canonical mapping should work from provided source records and configuration, not become another ERP client.
Derived facts and metrics depend on canonical SKU identity and local-warehouse selection semantics.
Metric derivation must consume normalized records, not raw ERP transport models.
Validation should be able to inspect the assembled response path end to end.
Validation needs direct access to raw ERP payloads for traceability and discrepancy analysis.
Validation should inspect contracts and assembled outputs without coupling itself to transport handlers.

invariants: ERP API calls exist only inside the ERP Source Gateway; no other module may introduce source-side integrations.
Canonical SKU resolution must happen before cross-endpoint joins or outward skuKey claims are treated as trustworthy.
Default-local-warehouse quantity may not enter the outward API response until local-warehouse selection semantics are explicit.
Metric derivation operates on normalized records and mapping decisions only; raw ERP payload math is not allowed in the API or application layer.
30-day outbound and 30/60/90-day sales must be traceable from raw ERP payloads through normalized records to final response values.
Validation and evidence are sidecar responsibilities and must not create an alternate production data path.
Export and non-API fallback are outside the default architecture and must not appear as routine parallel layers.
The architecture stays API-first and service-bounded; no dashboard or broad data-platform layer is part of v1.
